reviews.review_description:
Amazing game that to me feels like you are flying through the world of the original Tron movie.

Great graphics and great soundtrack.

reviews.review_title: How could this not be good
reviews.review_description: I played this on the PS2 back in the day and it was amazing then. I assumed it would be many times better on VR and boy - I was not wrong. It’s incredible!

I really liked the atmosphere of the game and the simple but special graphics crowned by the music. It requires little skill compared to Thumper. It's not too long and you can complete any level on the first or second try. But you can go on a wonderful journey in it. It reminded me of the retro atmosphere of old games. It's definitely worth it on sale.

reviews.review_title: An iconic game with a killer soundtrack
reviews.review_description: Rez is a classic, and being able to play it in VR is mindblowing. It is impressively immersive, and tries its best to not make you motion sick. Area X is a cool expansion of the original Rez formula.

reviews.review_title: A delightful blast from the past
reviews.review_description:
I used to play Rez on the PS2, Trance Vibrator and all. This took me back. 9 out of 10. Perfection would be a better 3D effect and fixing the weapons lock speed to match the beat like than the original. I’ll round up for you.

Devs: let’s see Child of Eden next, OK?

Thanks for all the delightful PLURy nostalgia.

I started my 15 minutes trial, and put it down after 5 minutes so I could purchase it in full. It’s exactly the reason why I bought a VR set. It’s immersion is brilliant, and with the soundtrack I’m not sure if i should sit down and play the game or be up dancing. Brilliant game guys, would highly recommend!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_title: Rez is made for VR, Even though it wasnt
reviews.review_description:
Been a big fan of Rez since buying it day 1 on the dreamcast, was always such a vibe and really felt ahead of its time... And this VR version proves it, as it feels like it was made with VR in mind.

The only way to explain Rez is its an on rails shooter, you aim and lock on to enemies and incoming projectiles, release and you fire to the beat of the amazing music.
Speaking of music, it's a big part of the game, and it ramps up as you progress through the levels before you take on a boss.

It's just an experience being in the rez world, zooming through as the wire frame world slides and spins around you.
Play with headphones on, and get in there!

Crazy to think this game is over 20 years old, and this VR version is so faithful to the original.

Experiences like this is what VR is for.
Get it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_title: Great Demo behind a bad ported old game
reviews.review_description:
Rez is an overall chill rhythm game, for those who already played it. You arent missing out much when playing this.  In addition, things sound a bit off compared to the original and the graphics are very poor not even upgraded. 

Area X was new, very beautiful and intriguing but it only lasted 15 to 20 mins and it was over. 

Old games being ported to VR is fine and all but this seems like a lazy port, compared to other titles like RE 4 VR where they at least upgraded the detailing and graphics here and there.

If this company can expand on Area X into a full fledge game lasting 8 hours or more at the same price though, I am game to purchase something like that.

Other than that, this game port is a rip-off.

Enjoyable for sure. Felt very immersed. Revisited a few times and probably played for 2 hours now. 25% off code below if interested

https://www.oculus.com/appreferrals/mattyd50/2610547289060480/?utm_source=2

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_title: A long time REZ fan... DISAPPOINTED!
reviews.review_description:
First and foremost, I am a musician of over 25 years. I know melody, rhythm, and harmony nearly innately at this point.

REZ is one of my top 10 games of all time, and has been for nearly 20 years now. I always hold it up as a masterclass in how to create art via the medium of video games. Besides the Dreamcast version, I have owned every single version of REZ that has been published. On the PS2, I even imported an official REZ Trance Vibrator from Japan back in 2005 (it plugged in to the PS2, and sat under your leg to vibrate with the beat in tandem with your controller). I bought REZ HD for the XBOX360, which was, unfortunately, a beautiful HD remaster marred by certain audio clips being out of tune with the rest of the game. I've even played Rez Infinite on the PS4 (where I didn't have any problems, and that is essentially the perfect version of this game).

But on the Oculus Quest 2, this game is completely off tempo for the entire experience. The entire concept behind the game is that it is supposed to induce a state similar to synesthesia in the player. The timing of the sounds, the loops, and the general rhythm with which the lock-on attack locks on is completely off. During the entire play-through, my little pulsating REZ hacker was on its own rhythm the entire time, my controller was vibrating off beat, and everything seemed to not be quantized to the rhythm like the game is supposed to be. Everything you do is supposed to create melody and rhythm ON THE BEAT (but at least fortunately in tune, in this version)

Ironically, this makes the game much easier, as you are able to achieve a MAX lock on well before 8 16th notes have passed, enabling you to spam the enemies with attacks much faster than you can in any other version of the game. Maybe the Oculus Quest 2 version of the game will become popular with speed runners, I guess? But for me, it lost a little bit of the magic when it just became a spammy clickfest rather than a groovy rhythmic shooter.

developer_response.body: Hrm, that sounds strange. It should not feel off. Please consider filing a support ticket with us via enhance-experience.zendesk.com so we can help you more directly.

reviews.review_description: Really unique game. At first it seems like the graphics are pretty basic, just being line art and dot art, but as you get into it, you feel immersed in a different world. The other mode, "Area X" is the closest I've felt to being inside the internet-world of A Ghost In The Shell, or The Matrix. It's a little weird because you use your head movement to steer, but it's such a surreal experience, it's fantastic. I wish the Area X part was longer, as it's pretty short, but it's well worth playing at least once.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_title: Video experience, disguised as a shooter
reviews.review_description:
Played both modes, I get the nostalgia from the Sega days, but honestly there’s not much to this “shooter”. First mode you pretty much wave your cursor over everything, lock and shoot and repeat. While your stick figure just floats there. 2nd mode which is much shorter,gives you a bit more control with flying wherever you point your head. Same point,lock,shoot, repeat. 

Things get so vague, I’m not even sure if I’m making a difference, this is more of a video experience disguised as a shooter , I really wanted to like this, because if it’s nostalgic roots, but I can’t shake the feeling I was scammed, do not buy this at full price

reviews.review_title: This shouldn't cost as much as it does.
reviews.review_description:
This game doesn't have enough gameplay to be worth the 20 dollar price tag. All you do is hold a button, aim at the enemies, and then let go of the button. It would be a bit more enjoyable if it wouldn't aim for you for the most part.

Also, I do like Area X a lot, but the camera controls are so bad that I can't play it without feeling sick.

reviews.review_title: Worst game I have ever purchased!
reviews.review_description: How this game has gotten so many good reviews boggles my mind. This game has no business being sold as a game for the OQ2. The graphics are so outdated it is actually sad. I took a chance on this game with a discount code and I really regret that decision. This game is literally worthless in the modern VR format. Take my advice, ignore the glowing reviews and save your money for a game that actually looks good on the OQ2 and actually uses current VR technology and modern visuals. Pathetic port. Two clickers down, Waaayyy down!

Pains me to say this as I was really looking forward to this. Got it with Tetris in the duo pack so I can’t refund it or I would....

Tetris has loads of replay value and after playing it for 2/3 hours I still haven’t completed all the levels.

With this, I read reviews saying it took a while to ramp up and only got hard after an hour into the game. Well...yes it did get more challenging. But then that was IT! I think I finished the 5 areas in about an hour or so, in one play session. I did not ‘die’ once? Is that normal? I hit about 97% of targets first time through leaving little replay value at all. 

In addition, I was really disappointed by the graphics on quest 2 actually. I mean it is quite immersive but a lot of wireframe and, not sure if it’s my headset or something, but I got a LOT of slowdown and frame rate issues. Whenever the bosses were on screen or a lot of enemies at once, I noticed an increasingly jerky frame rate. At times it felt a bit of a mess and I started to feel a little nausea just because of the jerky motion.

Also, let’s be clear, you don’t do an awful lot here. You just wave your controller around targeting up to 8 enemies at a time, let go the button, and boom they all get hit. Or madly squeeze the a button at everything, which after a while gets old and feels a bit like RSI on your finger. 

In saying all this, I did enjoy some segments immensely, and the soundtrack was great.  But the game shows it’s age and perhaps is trading on the name?

After I finished the main game I went straight onto Area X.

OMG this is more like it! Area X is what i imagined the whole game would be like. Super cool particle effects, a true sense of being able to move around the space rather than being on rails, and a way more immersive experience. This was the ‘wow’ moment for me and I loved it, with two caveats. 1. Like others say, it’s ridiculously short. I mean, again I completed it first go in about 15 minutes!! Wtf, come on devs you can do better than that! 2. The soundtrack IS mixed much lower than the main game. You have to turn the quest up to the max and it’s still not that loud.

So there you have it. I’m giving this 2 stars because I’m not too sure I will return and play it through again tbh. I’d show Area X as a quest demo to mates but that’s probably about it. And I can’t refund it sadly due to the duo pack policy. So £12 spent for maybe 1.5 hrs gameplay, seems pretty poor value for money.

Maybe the devs can update this with another Area X Level or two, as well as a 90hz Quest 2 update to the overall graphics to fix the frame rate issues? It seriously needs both these things to get a higher rating IMO.

Aging title, maybe unique in 2001, but that is not living up to its reputation 20 years later. Still interesting to discover in VR (I recommend waiting for a big sale), but not a must have experience nowadays.

Not sure if I’m missing something, sure the visuals are pretty, but is this game just press play and then tap the A button constantly until your thumb starts hurting then quit and play absolutely any other game that doesn’t involve constantly tapping one button until the end of time? I’ve heard it’s good on mushrooms but other than that it’s the worst game I’ve ever played on the quest.

Seeing reviews made me optimistic that this ancient port may have found some golden spot in the world of VR. 

While yes, it's pretty-ish.... There's really nothing innovative, challenging or even fun that I've found in this game. 

I suppose it's good that they stuffed it into a bundle, as it will reduce refunds... But I can't recommend anyone buy this on purpose for any other reason than you loved it 20 years ago and want that nostalgia.

I had high hopes for this game. I’ve heard of it but never played it. The screenshots and video make it look like it would be quite exciting and fun to play. However, the reality is somewhat different. Don’t get me wrong, it looks great but you basically do naff all whilst “playing”. Click a button now and then, move a stick now and then.... it’s just sooooo boring. No skills needed. Played it for bout an hour just waiting for the difficulty level to ramp up, wanted so much to be tested by the game so I could enjoy it but alas, I just sat on the floor eventually and gave up. Yawn. Maybe if it is developed further I will revisit this review, but as it stands, get it if it becomes free but don’t pay for it like I did.

reviews.review_title: The worst game I've had in quest 2
reviews.review_description: Rhythm game with no rhythm, has no right to be on the platform. Even nostalgia can't carry this one. Easiest 1 star review I've given.

It was on discount off so gave it a chance... No. Would not be worth it, even if instead of 2 Euros off it was 2 Euros. The game (so tired of this "experience" bs....) is rather generic, pointless, the feeling of being there (3D effects) is weak, and there seem to be invisible walls, so the camera may sometimes drift on its own to put you on your path. 
If there's one thing good about this entire Quest platform, it's the return and refund system.

Featuring 3d vector environments the graphics are pretty interesting. They also feature a few ( all too few) weird and wonderful vector creatures. That's the good news. Graphics 8.5/10
The sheer absence of gameplay is marked. It's as if gameplay was an afterthought. No movement control on the main game and just one controller - pretty much one button - for the game. You look around and press. That's not a game.
The additional game has, again, very interesting graphics with some thrust movement for your third-person character. It finishes after just a few minutes??!!?
Gameplay 1/10
Play it, return it, hope the Devs kickstart some replayable gameplay elements into future projects.

I have been contemplating returning this because;

A) Music is too low
B) 3D is not as pronounced as I would like for a VR experience, it's actually very little 3D.
C) Didn't get it at first... But now I get it!

Having said that, I have decided to keep the game.  

Overall this experience offers up a completely unique experience on Quest 2, it's refreshing and Area X is amazing!

Hope the Dev doesn't abandon this title and updates it with these suggestions above and hope they decide to do either a DLC or new releases of Area X expansion.
I cant believe how this has so many good reviews. save your money. i refunded after 20 mins play time.

Others must feel obligated to rate this five stars because it is considered a classic. If released today this game would be $5. It is also a bit dated. You will enjoy it for an hour or two and likely not play it much again.  

Also my rating reflects mostly upon gameplay.  There isn’t much in way of game here. Rez is more of an experience. 

I would give this a better rating if it weren’t 20 dollars.

A rail shooter relying solely on autolock with minimal graphics outside of exploding pixels (hope you're into wireframe).  Oh, and it's even a static rail, so don't worry about trying to dodge anything or have any other additional challenges from obstacles to add to the challenge.  Just blow up projectiles headed toward you.  I'm an EDM fan and the soundtrack is basically a toddler playing around trying to make techno on FruityLoops for their first time.  For 2 hours of game play, save your $22 and download any other free rail shooter you get w a game subscription like Panzer and sit a foot from your TV if you want to feel immersed.
It is stupid I wasted my money on it and it is so trash

If Rez wasn’t a classic, this game wouldn’t get half the current ratings.  There has been nothing added to make the game VR.  You can literally play sitting down and not move your head, not my idea of a vr game.  You might as well play your Dreamcast version on a big screen in the dark, it would be more satisfying.

An obvious enhancement would be to make the game first person rather than third person, feels like a key thing in VR?

Rather annoyed I simply didn’t request refund straight away, but that’s nostalgia for you.

If they make further updates (which I very very much doubt), I’ll review again. The game could be so much more, sadly I think they are just milking a cash cow.

One of my favorite games ever now in VR. Couldn't wait to press that buy button. Rail shooter where your attacks line up with the techno soundtrack. Turn that volume up and get lost in the crazy visuals!
Really cool game with an absolute banger of a soundtrack!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_description: I was looking for an arcade style game I could play seated. One I could get back to over and over again, for short or for long gaming sessions. Rez was the one I came across. Rez fulfils its purpose excellently, arcade in VR. If a classic arcade style game is what you want for your VR headset and you want to play it seated, Rez is the one. And as arcade shooter game it's best of its class. I'm just waiting for a comeback of the trance vibrator in a bluetooth version that connects to the Quest 2... 😉

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_title: People who get it love it
reviews.review_description:
It's an on rails shooter, aim by moving your head, lock with a, and release to fire
But it's musical, designed such that the hits are delayed so they hit in time with the music and your controllers vibration

The game has great art direction, and a story you have to figure out yourself

Reviewers saying this game is easy or short don't seem to realise that the games difficulty increases with skill level (more and better enemies and longer levels), and you see more of the game the better you do

There are many extra features, such as first person mode, boss rush mode, and extra levels, that can be unlocked

I would personally like an option for area x where your movement isn't tied to the direction you're looking, and is instead tied to the joy joystick

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_description:
Played this on other platforms in the past. 
This rendition is on another level.
Immersive & keeps you coming back for more.
Quite challenging too if you want to unlock options.
Good purchase.

This is my first game review! I am new to gaming and had never seen Rez prior to buying this. I started with area X briefly in a small guardian space and thought it was ok, read some of the 1 star reviews and had seriously thought of asking for a refund. I’m so glad i didn’t! I spent more time playing from the beginning and was blown away by the graphics which were awesome works of art. The gameplay was enjoyable as well; as an amateur I was able to satisfactorily adapt to hastily read instructions. For me, the value of the game is its beautiful and stunning graphics and accompanying music, which was such a treat... that alone made this well worth the money! i highly recommend this game especially if you value these types of experiences.

Not so challanging, yet not boring. Time went by without me noticing while blaying it. It is a sign of a goid experince!
Liked it very much

I first played this on psvr and it'll always be my favourite. It's weird getting used to aiming with your hand and not your face after learning the other way but it adds a new challenge.
Stunning visuals
Fantastic soundtrack
Fun gameplay

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_title: Rez, I wanna be inside you.
reviews.review_description: As someone that loved the earliest incarnation of Rez, this was a dream come true for me. A lot of the negative reviews here seem to bash the nostalgia of it all but that’s precisely why I’m here. I didn’t want a huge overhaul of a game I already felt was perfect, I simply wanted to feel more immersed in the game and that was absolutely achieved. I’ve always thought Rez was visually perfect and a piece of genius audio architecture. The gameplay here feels like the original except the player feels they are inside the game rather than observing it. That’s all I wanted: to be inside Rez. Mission accomplished. Area X is a gorgeous addition, like swimming in a space aquarium whilst on shrooms. The wiki for Rez says that the game was influenced by rave culture at the time. As an old head that partied in the early and mid 90s that makes total sense and was probably a large part of the appeal for me. I love Rez and the only change to the game I’d accept would be (maybe) a Hardkiss “Delusions of Grandeur” version. My brain would explode.

reviews.review_title: A pixel perfect VR ride of the OG!
reviews.review_description: As an enormous fan of the 360 console version, I must tip my hat to the devs here for respecting the creators work and not messing with perfection. Your gift to us is bringing this to VR where it always belonged but was way ahead of it's time! I cannot thank you enough for this. Please don't stop here, you know to what I am referring to!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_description: Gfx and music are excellent and the game plays awesome. If you liked the original then you will love the vr version. My new favourite game. Thankyou devs...

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We're glad to hear that you're enjoying the game.

reviews.review_title: The most amazing game I have ever pkayed
reviews.review_description: This game is incredible! Totally unique and immersive. Completely cyberdelic
